editions.summary: No new material verified oncology updates were added from the 20 July 2026 edition. The July 22 Morning Edition keeps the prior verified dashboard state visible after today's review.
No new material verified oncology updates were added from the 18 July 2026 edition. The July 20 Morning Edition keeps the prior verified dashboard state visible after today's review.
No new material verified oncology updates were added from the 16 July 2026 edition. The July 18 Morning Edition keeps the prior verified dashboard state visible after today's review.
No new verified oncology development cleared the bar on 16 July 2026, leaving Gedatolisib + Fulvestrant and the existing caution signals at the top of the briefing.
The 15 July 2026 morning edition captured the current verified oncology state and archived it as a dated briefing.
No new verified oncology development cleared the bar on 3 July 2026, leaving Tregzi Transplant Cell Therapy and the existing caution signals at the top of the briefing.
No new verified oncology development cleared the bar on 2 July 2026, leaving Tregzi Transplant Cell Therapy and the existing caution signals at the top of the briefing.
The 1 July 2026 weekly edition added FDA-approved Tregzi as a new hematologic-malignancy transplant dossier and refreshed the latest-approval logic around that regulator action.
The 30 June 2026 review published as a no-material-change edition: the dashboard held its late-June breast-cancer approvals while official-source checks did not justify a new promotion.
No new verified oncology development cleared the bar on 28 June 2026, leaving Sacituzumab govitecan + pembrolizumab and the existing caution signals at the top of the briefing.
No new verified oncology development cleared the bar on 27 June 2026, leaving Sacituzumab govitecan + pembrolizumab and the existing caution signals at the top of the briefing.
No new verified oncology development cleared the bar on 26 June 2026, leaving Sacituzumab govitecan + pembrolizumab and the existing caution signals at the top of the briefing.
The late-June edition captured two breast-cancer regulatory upgrades: a new palbociclib maintenance dossier and the FDA approval of ASCENT-04 / KEYNOTE-D19.
The 16 June 2026 morning edition captured the current verified oncology state and archived it as a dated briefing.
Morning briefing mode launched with the first watchlist-and-systems expansion, giving the dashboard a plain-language front page and explicit room for nonconforming oncology signals.

highlights.title: No new material verified updates
highlights.summary: The July 22 Morning Edition retains the prior verified dashboard state after no new material change was confirmed versus the 20 July 2026 edition.

highlights.title: Gedatolisib + Fulvestrant
highlights.summary: FDA approved gedatolisib with fulvestrant, with or without palbociclib, for PIK3CA-wild-type HR-positive/HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer after endocrine progression, adding a large progression-free-survival gain in a setting where many patients have already exhausted first-line endocrine options.

highlights.title: No new material verified updates
highlights.summary: The July 20 Morning Edition retains the prior verified dashboard state after no new material change was confirmed versus the 18 July 2026 edition.

highlights.title: Gedatolisib + Fulvestrant
highlights.summary: FDA approved gedatolisib with fulvestrant, with or without palbociclib, for PIK3CA-wild-type HR-positive/HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer after endocrine progression, adding a large progression-free-survival gain in a setting where many patients have already exhausted first-line endocrine options.

highlights.title: No new material dashboard item cleared the bar
highlights.summary: Official-source checks did not surface a post-28 June development strong enough for responsible inclusion.

highlights.title: ASCENT-04 remained the most recent verified approval
highlights.summary: The shortcut rail still pointed to the 24 June 2026 TNBC approval as the latest regulator-confirmed milestone in the dashboard.

highlights.title: Workforce and screening caution signals remained active
highlights.summary: The dashboard preserved system-level warnings and negative screening evidence rather than dropping them on quiet regulatory days.

highlights.title: No new oncology development cleared the bar
highlights.summary: Official-source checks did not justify promoting a newer oncology item into the briefing.

highlights.title: Sacituzumab govitecan + pembrolizumab remained the lead verified approval
Brand-name search now resolves newer dossiers
highlights.summary: Portfolio aliasing was expanded so users could find the new palbociclib entry through the `Ibrance` brand name.
